channel.title: # 🇱🇰 Sri Lanka This Week
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 98 English News Articles published between 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-14.

item.title: Sri Lanka secures staff-level deal for $700m tranche under IMF programme
item.description: The agreement covers the combined fifth and sixth reviews and would take total disbursements to about $2.4bn 💰, pending consideration by the Executive Board. Programme documents note steady macro stabilisation, with low single‑digit inflation and stronger reserves, while underscoring that completion hinges on continued reform implementation.

item.title: Colombo Stock Exchange logs a broad rally with ASPI up 4.21% and S&P SL20 up 4.42% on April 8
item.description: Turnover reached Rs. 6.61bn with 11 negotiated crossings, including sizeable prints in banking and diversified holdings. The rupee was quoted around Rs. 315.10/25 per US dollar in spot trade, and dealers reported lower bond yields across key tenors, as daily share volume exceeded 240m units.

item.title: Government unveils Rs. 2.16bn modernisation of Colombo Fort Railway Station with ADB support
item.description: The 15‑month upgrade introduces automated ticketing, lifts and overhead bridges for accessibility, and new passenger facilities configured to international standards. Works are being carried out under the Transport, Highways and Urban Development portfolio to reconfigure the island’s busiest node as a safer, technology‑enabled hub.

item.title: Commercial Bank teams up with NCG Express to roll out fully digital ticketing across private buses
item.description: Conductors are being equipped with handheld devices to accept contactless cards, wallets and QR scans, with instant confirmations and automated settlement over the acquiring network. Initial routes have commenced under a structured rollout, enabling cash‑free fares and producing auditable records that standardise high‑volume, everyday transactions.

item.title: Train services on the Northern Line resume after cyclone repairs funded by an Indian grant
item.description: Track restoration under a $5m package was completed ahead of Avurudu, reconnecting stretches damaged by Ditwah and re‑establishing through movements to key northern towns. The reopening reinstates a critical long‑distance corridor for passengers and freight, easing road congestion during the festive peak.

item.title: Hayleys Solar completes 800kW rooftop installation for LSEG’s Sri Lanka campus
item.description: The six‑building array of 1,412 high‑efficiency panels is expected to generate roughly 1,250 MWh a year, covering close to 80% of daytime demand and avoiding about 850 tonnes of CO₂ annually ⚡. Engineering included extensive underground cabling across an active site, executed over two months with a zero‑accident record.

item.title: Vidullanka acquires 100% of Bhoruka Power Lanka, adding the 4.5MW Gurugoda mini‑hydro to its portfolio
item.description: The buyer now counts 26 operating assets after the Aranayake plant joins a fleet that spans hydro, solar and emerging storage projects. Recent activity includes four ground‑mounted solar commissions, a tender win for a 50MW wind project and a 10MW BESS, plus the lowest‑bid outcome for a mini‑hydro in Fiji.

item.title: Control Union Sri Lanka sets two global firsts with inaugural Deforestation‑Free Standard certificates
item.description: The auditor issued the maiden DFS credential to Hapugasthenna Plantations PLC, covering eleven rubber estates assessed for legality, labour safeguards and land‑use verification aligned to EU rules. A subsequent Group Certificate to Samson Rubber Industries created a shared structure that brings smallholders under one compliance umbrella while preserving traceability to plot.

item.title: Motor Traffic Commissioner General further remanded to 20 April over alleged fraudulent vehicle registration
item.description: The suspect was produced before the Pilessa Magistrate’s Court in Kurunegala, extending custody in a case involving an altered chassis on a cab. Investigators from the Criminal Investigation Department arrested the official on 27 March and have filed aiding‑and‑abetting charges tied to the registration process.

item.title: Colombo Dockyard announces board changes as Capt. (Retd.) Jagmohan becomes Chairman after MDL takes 51%
item.description: The shipbuilder confirmed Thimira S. Godakumbura continuing as Managing Director/CEO, with new non‑executive appointments including Vish Govindasamy and Senthilverl Nandhanan. The Indian majority owner’s nominee directors add naval architecture and yard leadership credentials to the boardroom, aligning governance with a post‑acquisition growth mandate.
